title: Facilities Executive
description:
Office Management

  • Manage staff seat and space allocation; conduct regular inspections.
  • Act as travel coordinator for inbound and outbound guests (hotel bookings, flight and airport transfer arrangements).
  • Manage card access (issuance of access cards, termination of access cards, granting access, etc)
  • Oversee key management, including issuance, return, and protocol review.
  • Support asset and waste disposal administration (such as PC, monitor, CPU, etc)
  • Administer asset management, including receiving and disposal of FM-related assets.
  • Draft and disseminate communication circulars for facility/building matters
  • Coordinate and supervise Move, Add & Change (MAC) projects.
  • Maintain the Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS) for facility management services.
  • Able to perform Microsoft Office Analysis


Hard & Soft Services / Contractor Management

  • Provide monthly reporting to track maintenance contractor performance, outstanding work, and follow-up actions; assess and trend performance indicators
  • Support contract management for sub-contractors, monitor scheduled performance requirements, and report deviations.
  • Plan and  wok with client and compile 52-week Planned Preventive Maintenance (PPM) schedules; track, update, and report progress monthly.
  • Raise and approve Permit to Work for contractor access; track issuance.
  • Ensure contractor submission of safety documents (Method Statement, Risk Assessment).
  • Update client FM representative weekly on planned works.


Soft Services

  • Manage security, cleaning, pest control, plants maintenance, and sanitary bins

Operations Support

  • Act as first responder for facility-related issues; track recovery and report monthly.
  • Respond to office feedback on faults/repairs; coordinate root cause analysis and follow-up measures.
  • Facilitate site access for maintenance activities; supervise contractors for scheduled, ad hoc, and breakdown works.
  • Facilitate building management access for meter readings and servicing.
  • Record and analyze energy consumption; investigate anomalies and recommend conservation measures.
  • Manage annual power shutdown activities, including scheduling, coordination, and post-event reporting.
  • Assess site/system conditions; develop and implement best practices and sustainment plans.
  • Maintain records/logs of all facilities-related documents and track status.
  • Coordinate office moves, A&A works, and renovation projects.
  • Manage handover/takeover from project to FM.

·

postedCompany.description: Savills Property Management, became a subsidiary after Savills Singapore acquired a stake in CKH Strata Management and merged their property management business.  The founding Director, Mr Chan Kok Hong’s vision was to provide his clients with the very best strata management advice and service, on par with international standards but with local knowledge and expertise. This vision can now be taken to the next level by tapping into Savills Global network and expertise where Savills has over 1 billion sq ft of properties under management.  Savills Property Management Pte Ltd has set itself apart from its competition through professionalism, service excellence, efficiency, integrity, strategic application of information technology and concern for the welfare of the residents.  Today, it has become the largest privately held strata management company with a staff strength of over 500 and services over 160 clients, including landmark buildings such as DUO, Shopping/Office/Hotel Complex, orchardgateway, orchardgateway@emrald, 20 Collyer Quay, Sim Lim Square and Singapore larger condominiums such as The Interlace, Parc Oasis, Signature Park and Costal Del Sol.   At Savills Property Management, we are committed to developing, supporting and empowering our employees.  We are currently seeking suitable candidates with the passion for strata management to join us.
postedCompany.name: SAVILLS PROPERTY MANAGEMENT PTE. LTD.
